component.name: Row Card
component.description: Attention, roster, and empty-state rows: 353px-wide rounded cards with optional leading count/icon, two-line title stack, and trailing metric or CTA. Padding `Spacing/20` × `Spacing/16`, radius `Radius/16`, shadow `0 1px 2px rgba(0,0,0,0.05)`.

variants.unsortedCaptures: Brand tint surface (`Brand/PrimaryBg`) + `Brand/PrimaryStroke` border; leading count well `Brand/Primary`; trailing link tone `Brand/Primary`.
variants.incompleteJob: Warning surface + stroke; subtitle line `Semantic/Status/Error/Text` (Missing: …); trailing `Fix →`.
variants.pendingPayment: Warning surface + stroke; leading well `Semantic/Status/Warning/Label` + muted number; trailing amount `Typography/Metric` + warning label tone.
variants.rankedJobWithMetric: White surface; neutral rank well; trailing `Semantic/Status/Success/Text` metric (e.g. $/hr).
variants.simpleJob: White surface; title + subtitle only (no lead, no trail).
variants.quickActionDefault: Solid `Brand/Primary` fill; leading `Brand/PrimaryHover` well + plus; title/subtitle in light tints (`Foundation/Text/Muted`, `Brand/PrimaryStroke`).
variants.activityCard: White surface; 28px activity well (10% `Brand/Primary` tint); clock glyph; session title + time.
variants.emptyWithButton: White surface; column: empty message + pill button (`Foundation/Surface/Subtle`, `Typography/LABEL`). Taller frame (90px).

figmaLayerNames.componentSet: Row Card (vertical stack of variant masters, 16px gap)

findings: 2026-04-08: Component set `786:28` — `VERTICAL` + `NO_WRAP`, 16px `itemSpacing`; `clipsContent` false on set and variant masters. Structural frames renamed to `row-card-*` kebab-case: main/trailing split where two columns; `row-card-leading` + `row-card-leading-value` or `row-card-leading-icon`; activity uses `row-card-leading-row` → `row-card-leading-icon`; title stack uses `row-card-title-line` / `row-card-subtitle-line`, incomplete uses `row-card-missing-detail`; empty states use `row-card-empty-message` and `row-card-empty-action`. No `flatten()` in automation. React `RowCard.tsx` mirrors layers via `data-name`.
